mechanism: Immigration swings (±2M/year in US) directly affect labor availability that determines whether EBITDA synergies embedded in addbacks can be realized. High immigration may temporarily validate addbacks; restriction may accelerate their failure.
regime_dependence: Strongest for labor-intensive sectors with high immigrant workforce share. Three-channel credit transmission (RMBS, ABS, leveraged margins) creates correlated stress. Policy swings can rapidly shift the outlook.
